preamble: The shift from a voiced velar stop [g] to a voiced palatal stop [ɟ] is the voiced counterpart to [k] > [c]. This stage is found in the historical development of Slavic and Romance, and is preserved as a distinct phoneme in languages like Hungarian.

examples.note: Hungarian has a phonemic voiced palatal stop /ɟ/ (written 'gy').
Intermediate stage in the First Slavic Palatalization before [dʒ] or [ʒ].
Pre-affrication stage of Latin /g/ before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
